prose:
The Fukui reactivity analysis for phenol was completed using Density Functional Theory (DFT). The calculation was performed at the tier 'standard' level, which corresponds to the B3LYP functional and the def2-TZVP basis set, utilizing density fitting. The calculation was run in the gas phase on the user-provided geometry, assuming a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1. The PySCF software package was used for the computation.

The results indicate the sites most susceptible to electrophilic and nucleophilic attack:
- **Most nucleophilic site** (highest f-): Atom 1 (O) with a value of 0.16201.
- **Most electrophilic site** (highest f+): Atom 3 (C) with a value of 2.1028.

The calculation converged successfully and the engine reported no warnings.

### Fukui Indices per Atom

| Atom Index | Symbol | f+ (Electrophilic Attack) | f- (Nucleophilic Attack) | f0 (Radical Attack) |
|------------|--------|---------------------------|--------------------------|---------------------|
| 1          | O      | -0.99514                  | **0.16201**              | -0.41656            |
| 2          | C      | 0.39933                   | 0.04877                  | 0.22405             |
| 3          | C      | **2.10283**               | 0.07097                  | 1.08690             |
| 4          | C      | 1.61968                   | 0.07050                  | 0.84509             |
| 5          | C      | -0.34201                  | 0.02670                  | -0.15765            |
| 6          | C      | -0.28911                  | 0.02095                  | -0.13408            |
| 7          | C      | 1.30798                   | 0.12352                  | 0.71575             |
| 8          | H      | -0.73392                  | 0.08232                  | -0.32580            |
| 9          | H      | -0.27466                  | 0.08815                  | -0.09325            |
| 10         | H      | -0.91706                  | 0.08111                  | -0.41798            |
| 11         | H      | -0.62992                  | 0.08049                  | -0.27471            |
| 12         | H      | -0.29592                  | 0.09350                  | -0.10121            |
| 13         | H      | 0.04790                   | 0.05101                  | 0.04946             |
